question:
Directions:Read the information carefully and

answer the questions given below.

At a reunion party, eight friends named P, Q, R,

S, T, U, V and W sit together on a round table

facing the center. Interestingly, all eight friends

are pursuing bachelor’s degrees in different

subjects namely Mathematics, English, Business

studies, Polity, Physics, Chemistry, Mythology

and Hindi. They are not necessarily seated in

the mentioned order. R sits adjacent to S who is

studying Business studies. Q is studying Polity

and does not sit adjacent to R or S. U is sitting

fifth to the left of P and is studying Physics. One

who is studying Chemistry sits immediate left to

U. V sits opposite to P. Neither W nor T is

immediate neighbor of V. One who is studying

Hindi sits fifth to the left of V, who is learning

Mythology. T is studying English and sits next to

the one who is studying Hindi.

What does the person study who is sitting third

to the left of R?

question:
Study the following information carefully and

answer the questions given below:

Seven persons i.e. Z, Y, X, W, V, U and T of

different ages i.e. 6, 15, 19, 23, 30, 45, 60 years

are sitting in a circular table facing towards the

center. Note- No two consecutive letters are in

alphabetical order. Person’s age must not be a

factor or a multiple of the sum of the ages of

their immediate neighbors. X sits immediate to

the right of the person whose age is 15. One

person sits between V and X . One person sits

between Z and the person whose age is 6 . Age

of V is twice the age of the person who sits 2nd

to the right of V. Di erence between the age of

the immediate neighbors of T is more than 14.

Age of U must not be multiple of 10. Sum of the

ages of immediate neighbors of X is 34. Y is

younger than Z.

What is the age of Z?

question:
Directions: Study the following data carefully

and answer the questions accordingly.

Eight people A, B, C, D, E, F, G and H are sitting

around a circular table with equal distance

between each other but not necessarily in the

same order. All are facing inside the circle. G

and A are immediate neighbors of each other.

There are three people sitting between A and F.

C sits second to the left of F. The number of

people sitting between G and C is the same

number of people sitting between C and E. B

sits second to the right of D, who does not sit

near to F.

Four of the following five are alike in a certain

way and so form a group. Which one does not

belong to that group?

Varun is an immediate neighbor of the one

who is second to the left of Akhil

Varun is not an immediate neighbor of

Manish and Suresh

circle.

Directions: Study the following data carefully

and answer the questions accordingly.

Eight people A, B, C, D, E, F, G and H are sitting

around a circular table with equal distance

between each other but not necessarily in the

same order. Four people are facing inside and

four people are facing outside. D sits second to

the left of G. There are two people sitting

between D and A. A does not sit near to G. A

sits opposite to H. E sits second to the right of H,

who is facing the opposite direction to G. C sits

third to the left of E and both are facing in the

same direction. B sits second to the right of C. F

sits third to the left of B, who faces outside the

circle.

Who sits second to the right of F ?

Directions: Study the following data carefully

and answer the questions accordingly.

Eight people P, Q, R, S, T, U, V and W are sitting

around a circular table with equal distance

between each other but not necessarily in the

same order. Four people are facing inside and

four people are facing outside. V sits third to the

right of W. The immediate neighbor of W faces

the opposite direction as W faces. R sits just

right of W. T sits third to the left of R, both are

facing opposite directions to each other. S sits

third to the right of R, and sits opposite to U. P

sits second to the left of V, and both are facing

opposite directions to each other. S and Q sit

near each other and both are facing the same

direction to each other. U face inside the circle.

As many people sitting between P and V as

many between Q and _ ?
question_html:
Directions: Study the following data carefully

and answer the questions accordingly.

Eight people P, Q, R, S, T, U, V and W are sitting

around a circular table with equal distance

between each other but not necessarily in the

same order. Four people are facing inside and

four people are facing outside. V sits third to the

right of W. The immediate neighbor of W faces

the opposite direction as W faces. R sits just

right of W. T sits third to the left of R, both are

facing opposite directions to each other. S sits

third to the right of R, and sits opposite to U. P

sits second to the left of V, and both are facing

opposite directions to each other. S and Q sit

near each other and both are facing the same

direction to each other. U face inside the circle.

As many people sitting between P and V as

many between Q and _ ?

question:
Directions: Study the following data carefully

and answer the questions accordingly.

Certain numbers of persons are sitting around a

circular table such that the persons whose

names
start with a consonant are
facing

outward while the persons whose names start

with a vowel are facing inward. Each of the

persons had a different number of candies with

them which are consecutive multiples of 2

starting from R clockwise. R had 8 candies. They

decided to shuffle the candies among them

such that each person gives half of his candies

to the person sitting second to his right. The

following information is after the candies have

been shuffled:

A is sitting second to the left of L. E is an

immediate neighbour of R. C has 34 candies.

One person is sitting in between C and L. Three

persons are sitting in between E and C. K is

sitting to the immediate left of Z. K has 36

candies. Three persons are sitting in between K

and S. Y is sitting fifth to the left of E. The total

number of candies with the persons in between

E and S is 17. A and E are the only persons at the

table whose names start with vowels.

How many candies does L have after shuffling?

questionHtml:
Directions: Study the following data carefully

and answer the questions accordingly.

Certain numbers of persons are sitting around a

circular table such that the persons whose

names
start with a consonant are
facing

outward while the persons whose names start

with a vowel are facing inward. Each of the

persons had a different number of candies with

them which are consecutive multiples of 2

starting from R clockwise. R had 8 candies. They

decided to shuffle the candies among them

such that each person gives half of his candies

to the person sitting second to his right. The

following information is after the candies have

been shuffled:

A is sitting second to the left of L. E is an

immediate neighbour of R. C has 34 candies.

One person is sitting in between C and L. Three

persons are sitting in between E and C. K is

sitting to the immediate left of Z. K has 36

candies. Three persons are sitting in between K

and S. Y is sitting fifth to the left of E. The total

number of candies with the persons in between

E and S is 17. A and E are the only persons at the

table whose names start with vowels.

How many persons have more candies than

they had previously?
explanation:
They had candies with them which are

consecutive multiples of 2 starting from R

clockwise. R had 8 candies. Hence, the number

of candies previously was 8, 10, 12, 14, ... After

shuffling, a person will have (n + m)/2 candies,

where n = no. of candies he had and m = no. of

candies he received.

A and E are the only persons at the table whose

names start with vowels and hence facing

inward.

Also, most of the persons (except A and E) are

facing outward and will give candies to the one

sitting second to his right.

C has 34 candies. Three persons are sitting in

between E and C. E is an immediate neighbour

of R.

One person is sitting in between C and L. A is

sitting second to the left of L. So, cases 1(i) and

1(ii) are invalid and also, L will not get A's candies

as A is facing inward. As C will get L's candies,

so for C, (m +n)/2 = 34 and n = m + 4. From this,

we get, m = 32, n = 36. Hence, L will now have

32/2 = 16 candies only (half of his candies as he

won't get candies from anyone else). Hence,

original no. of candies with A = L - 4 = 32 - 4 =

28. Now, A must-have 28/2 + (28 - 4)/2 = 26

candies.

question_html:
Directions: Study the following data carefully

and answer the questions accordingly.

Certain numbers of persons are sitting around a

circular table such that the persons whose

names
start with a consonant are
facing

outward while the persons whose names start

with a vowel are facing inward. Each of the

persons had a different number of candies with

them which are consecutive multiples of 2

starting from R clockwise. R had 8 candies. They

decided to shuffle the candies among them

such that each person gives half of his candies

to the person sitting second to his right. The

following information is after the candies have

been shuffled:

A is sitting second to the left of L. E is an

immediate neighbour of R. C has 34 candies.

One person is sitting in between C and L. Three

persons are sitting in between E and C. K is

sitting to the immediate left of Z. K has 36

candies. Three persons are sitting in between K

and S. Y is sitting fifth to the left of E. The total

number of candies with the persons in between

E and S is 17. A and E are the only persons at the

table whose names start with vowels.

Which of the following is correct?

I. There are 19 persons in the arrangement.

II. Only L did not receive candies during

shuffling.

III. Two persons have an odd number of

candies after shuffling.

options.text: None of I, II and III
options.html: None of I, II and III

optionsHtml: None of I, II and III
explanation:
They had candies with them which are

consecutive multiples of 2 starting from R

clockwise. R had 8 candies. Hence, the number

of candies previously was 8, 10, 12, 14, ... After

shuffling, a person will have (n + m)/2 candies,

where n = no. of candies he had and m = no. of

candies he received.

A and E are the only persons at the table whose

names start with vowels and hence facing

inward.

Also, most of the persons (except A and E) are

facing outward and will give candies to the one

sitting second to his right.

explanation:
Starting Point: Start with placing O, P, and T to

form two cases initially.

Clues:

1. O and P sit adjacent to each other.

2. T sits 8m to the left of P, who is CEO of Wipro.

3. The distance between T and R is 4m, when

considered from left of T.

Inferences:

From clue 1, we get O sits either immediate left

or right of P.

From clue 2, we get T sits either 3 to the left or

3 to the right of O.

From clue 3, we get R sits either 3 to the right

or immediate right of O.

Case I: When O sits immediate left of P.

Case II: When O sits immediate right of P.

Clues:

1. The distance between S and R is 6m, from

either side of the table.

2. S doesn’t sit adjacent to T.

3. U sits second to the left of Q, who is CEO of

P&G.

4. The person, who is CEO of TCS, sits second to

the left of U.

Inferences:

From clue 1 and clue 2, we reject Case I as we

can’t place S. S sits immediate left of P.

From clue 3, we get U sits immediate left of T. Q

sits immediate right of T. V sits immediate left of

S.

question:
Study the following information carefully and

answer the questions accordingly.

A total of eight people(A, I, J, L, M, N, Q and T)

are playing cards. They are sitting around a

circular table, facing at the center. When they

got to the last round, they all left with one card

in their hands viz. K, A, J, 10, 9, 7, 5 and 2, but not

in the same order. The people whose names

start with a vowel have face cards.

The one who has 5 doesn't sit next to the one

who has A. J has 9. Two people sit between M

and T. The one who has J is an immediate

neighbor of the one who has K. Two people sit

between the one who has A and L. L sits

opposite to the one who has 2. M does not

have a prime number card. I is neither an

immediate neighbor of L nor the one who has A.

Two people sit between the one who has 7 and

J. J and I are neighbors. J and L are not

neighbors. L doesn't have 7. The one who has 7

is not an immediate neighbor of L. A sits third to

the right of the one who has 7. N doesn't have A.

Who has 2?

circular table, facing at the center. When they

got to the last round, they all left with one card

in their hands viz. K, A, J, 10, 9, 7, 5 and 2, but not

in the same order. The people whose names

start with a vowel have face cards.

The one who has 5 doesn't sit next to the one

who has A. J has 9. Two people sit between M

and T. The one who has J is an immediate

neighbor of the one who has K. Two people sit

between the one who has A and L. L sits

opposite to the one who has 2. M does not

have a prime number card. I is neither an

immediate neighbor of L nor the one who has A.

Two people sit between the one who has 7 and

J. J and I are neighbors. J and L are not

neighbors. L doesn't have 7. The one who has 7

is not an immediate neighbor of L. A sits third to

the right of the one who has 7. N doesn't have A.

Who sits opposite N?
explanation:
1. Two people sit between the one who has A

and L.

2. L sits opposite to the one who has 2.

CASE 1

question:
Directions:Study the following data carefully

and answer the questions accordingly.

Ap, Bq, Cr, Ds, Et, and Fu are six girls who sit

around a circular table (not always in the same

order) facing the centre. They have 14, 17, 18, 20,

23, and 24 barbies, but not always in the same

order. Any two adjacent girls are the same

distance between them.

Ap sits 2nd to the right of Bq. Ap has 1 barbie

less than Bq. Cr doesn’t have 17 barbies. Fu

neither has 20 barbies nor 23 barbies. Ds faces

the girl who has the 2nd lowest number of

barbies and the sum of their barbies is not 40.

The girl, who sits 2nd to the right of Ds, has the

lowest barbies. Fu has more than 17 barbies. Fu

sits to the immediate right of Cr.

How many barbies Et has?

Ap, Bq, Cr, Ds, Et, and Fu are six girls who sit

around a circular table (not always in the same

According to the common solution,

Et has 14 barbies.

Hence, the correct answer is option A.

question:
Directions: Study the following data carefully

and answer the questions accordingly.

At a reception party, eight persons Nita, Sita,

Gita, Babita, Lalita, Mita, Omita and Rita are

sitting around a circular table. Four of them are

facing inside while the rest are facing outside

the Centre. Each of them is wearing different

types of dresses such as Kurti, Anarkali, Suit,

lehenga, Saree, Jeans, Gown and One piece

not necessary in the same order.

Only one person is sitting between Nita and the

one who is wearing Lehenga. Babita is sitting

second to the left of the one who is wearing

Suit. The one who is wearing jeans is sitting

opposite Omita. Gita is sitting third to the right

of the one who is wearing Anarkali, the one who

is wearing Anarkali faces inside the Centre. Only

three persons are sitting between Gita and Sita.

The one who is wearing Gown is sitting on the

immediate left of Sita. The one who is wearing

Lehenga is third to the left of Omita. Omita is

neither an immediate neighbor of Sita nor of

Gita. Mita is wearing One piece. Mita is not an

immediate neighbor of Nita. The one who is

wearing Saree is an immediate neighbor of

Mita. The one who is wearing Jeans is not a

neighbor of Lalita. Nita is not wearing Gown.

Four of the following forms a group, find which

of the following does not belongs to that

group?

question:
Directions: Study the following data carefully

and answer the questions accordingly.

Eight Persons viz, P, Q, R, S, T, U, V, and W are

sitting on a Circular table and all of them are

faces inside but not necessarily in the same

order. Further, it is known that all persons have

either different or the same heights.

U, who is immediate taller than T and sits four

places away from P. S, who is not immediate

taller than Q. Only two persons sit between P

and Q. Q’s height is two persons taller than W,

who is taller than T. P sits two places away from

V. S, who is taller than Q, sits immediate left of V.

The number of persons sitting between P and V

is the same as the number of persons sitting

between P and the one who is immediate

shorter than U. R’s height is more than U’s but

lesser than P, who is not the tallest. Q, who is

not the tallest, is shorter than at least two

persons. The one who is taller than W but lesser

than Q sits third to the right of U. R’s height is

not lesser than W's. V’s height is not smaller

than Q and not sits second to the left of P. S is

not the tallest.

In which of the following options persons sits

opposite to each other?

facing the center. C is sitting second to the left

neither likes William nor Ramires. C does not like

Messi.

the one who likes Ronaldo and both are facing

the same direction. M neither likes William nor

Ramires. Therefore, Case-1 gets eliminated. I

likes Xavi.

question:
Study the following information carefully and

answer the questions accordingly.

Ten persons namely A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H, I and J

are sitting around a circular table with an equal

number of persons facing outside and inside

and they like different vehicles viz, Auto, Car,

Bike, Scooter, Activa, Aeroplane, Truck, Jet, Ship

and Train but not necessarily in the same order.

No two adjacent students face the same

direction.

C sits third to the right of D, who is second to

the left of B. E sits second to the left of D, who

faces inside. B sits second to the left of the one

who likes Car. The one who likes Truck is two

places away from the one who likes Car. B

doesn’t like Truck. A likes Car and sits second to

the left of F, who likes Truck. The one who likes

Auto sits second to the right of C. G doesn’t like

Auto. Only one person sits between G and C,

who like Aeroplane. E and D like Jet and Ship

respectively. Only one person sits between H

and G. J sits opposite the one who likes Car

and fourth to the left of the one who likes Bike.

H, who likes Train. B doesn’t like scooter.

Four from the following five are related in a

certain way and hence form a group. Find the

one which doesn’t belong to that group?

explanation:
They had candies with them which are

consecutive multiples of 2 starting from R

clockwise. R had 8 candies. Hence, the number

of candies previously was 8, 10, 12, 14, ... After

shuffling, a person will have (n + m)/2 candies,

where n = no. of candies he had and m = no. of

candies he received.

A and E are the only persons at the table whose

names start with vowels and hence facing

inward.

Also, most of the persons (except A and E) are

facing outward and will give candies to the one

sitting second to his right.

C has 34 candies. Three persons are sitting in

between E and C. E is an immediate neighbour

of R.

One person is sitting in between C and L. A is

sitting second to the left of L. So, cases 1(i) and

1(ii) are invalid and also, L will not get A's candies

as A is facing inward. As C will get L's candies,

so for C, (m +n)/2 = 34 and n = m + 4. From this,

we get, m = 32, n = 36. Hence, L will now have

32/2 = 16 candies only (half of his candies as he

won't get candies from anyone else). Hence,

original no. of candies with A = L - 4 = 32 - 4 =

28. Now, A must-have 28/2 + (28 - 4)/2 = 26

candies.

files is not 40.

3. The employee, who sits 2nd to the right of

employee D, has lowest files.

explanation:
1. Employee A sits second to the right of

employee B. Employee A has 1 file less than

employee B.

2. Employee D faces the person who has the

2nd lowest number of files and the sum of their

files is not 40.

3. The employee, who sits 2nd to the right of

employee D, has lowest files.

4. Employee F has more than 17 files and

employee C, who doesn’t have 17 files, sits

immediate left of employee F

5. Employee F neither has 20 files nor 23 files, so

case II is rejected.

6. Employee C sits to the immediate left of

employee F.

According to the common solution,

The employee, who has 20 files sit between

employee F and employee B if counted from

the right of employee F.

Option B, is hence the correct answer.
